name: Code Review
description: Systematic code review focusing on correctness, security, performance, and style
content:
# Code Review

Start from the diff and stated intent. Verify logic, invariants, and failure modes before nitpicks. Flag security (authz, injection, secrets), concurrency, and performance hotspots with evidence. Prefer actionable comments: what, why, and a concrete fix. Ensure tests/docs align with behavior changes.

End with a short severity-sorted summary.

name: Refactoring
description: Safe structural improvements without changing observable behavior
content:
# Refactoring

Preserve behavior: add characterization tests or narrow scopes first. Rename and extract in small steps; avoid mixing refactors with feature work. Watch for API breakage, serialization, and implicit contracts. After each step, compile and run targeted tests. Document notable design shifts only when they affect consumers.

name: Debugging
description: Systematic root-cause analysis from symptoms to minimal fix
content:
# Debugging

Reproduce reliably; capture inputs, versions, and timestamps. Form hypotheses and falsify them with logs or breakpoints. Bisect recent changes and isolate variables. Prefer fixes that address causes, not symptoms—then add a regression test. If blocked, document unknowns and the smallest safe mitigation.

name: Test-Driven Development
description: Red-green-refactor cycles with tight feedback loops
content:
# TDD

Write a failing test that expresses the desired behavior boundary. Implement the minimum to pass; refactor with green tests only. Keep tests fast, deterministic, and readable—avoid testing implementation details. When requirements shift, update tests first. Stop when coverage matches risk, not arbitrary percentages.

name: Code Generation
description: Produce idiomatic code from specs with review-ready output
content:
# Code Generation

Translate requirements into interfaces and data shapes first. Generate small, reviewable chunks with consistent style and error handling. Never embed secrets; parameterize config. Cross-check against project conventions and existing patterns. After generation, run formatters, linters, and compile—then trim dead code.

name: Pair Programming
description: Driver/navigator rhythm for shared problem solving
content:
# Pair Programming

Agree on goal and constraints before typing. Driver narrates intent; navigator watches for design drift and edge cases. Swap roles on natural breakpoints. Prefer thin slices: make it work, then clear, then fast. Capture decisions briefly so the team can follow rationale later.

name: Dependency Audit
description: Evaluate third-party packages for risk, licenses, and updates
content:
# Dependency Audit

Inventory direct and transitive deps with pinned ranges where possible. Check CVEs, maintenance signals, and license compatibility. Prefer fewer, well-supported libraries. Plan upgrades in batches with rollback notes. Document exceptions and compensating controls when you must accept risk.

name: Linting & Standards
description: Enforce consistent style and static checks across the codebase
content:
# Linting & Standards

Align rules with team defaults; avoid noisy churn. Fix auto-fixables in dedicated commits. For new rules, migrate gradually with baselines or scoped paths. Explain non-obvious suppressions with tickets. Pair lint config changes with CI enforcement and editor integration so drift is caught early.

name: API Design
description: Design clear, evolvable interfaces for humans and machines
content:
# API Design

Start from consumer journeys and failure cases. Prefer explicit types, pagination, and idempotency keys where needed. Version thoughtfully; avoid breaking changes without migration paths. Document error models and auth requirements. Validate designs with examples and compatibility checks before wide rollout.

name: Error Handling
description: Predictable failures, observability, and user-safe messaging
content:
# Error Handling

Classify errors: expected vs unexpected, retryable vs fatal. Propagate context without leaking secrets. Log structured fields (code, correlation id, cause chain). Translate to user-visible messages that are honest and actionable. Add tests for failure paths, not only happy paths.

name: Logging Strategy
description: Structured logs, levels, and correlation for operability
content:
# Logging

Use stable event names and consistent key names. Default to info for milestones; debug behind flags in prod. Include correlation/request ids across services. Avoid logging PII or secrets—redact aggressively. Define retention and sampling for high-volume paths. Verify queries/dashboards match on-call needs.

name: Performance Profiling
description: Measure first, optimize hotspots with evidence
content:
# Performance Profiling

Establish a reproducible scenario and baseline metrics (p50/p95, CPU, memory). Profile before micro-optimizing; attribute time to functions/IO. Fix dominant costs; re-measure after each change. Watch for regressions in cold start and memory churn. Document benchmarks and test conditions.

name: Type Safety
description: Leverage static types to prevent entire bug classes
content:
# Type Safety

Model invalid states as unrepresentable when practical. Prefer explicit unions over booleans plus conventions. Narrow at boundaries (IO, JSON) and trust types internally. Avoid unsafe casts; encode invariants in types or validators. Use generics to remove duplication without erasing intent.

name: Inline Documentation
description: Comments and docstrings that explain why, not what
content:
# Inline Documentation

Document non-obvious rationale, tradeoffs, and invariants—not obvious code. Keep comments near decisions they justify; delete stale notes. For public APIs, summarize behavior, errors, and threading contracts. Prefer examples for complex usage. Treat docs as code: review and test snippets.

name: Code Architecture
description: Module boundaries, coupling, and long-term maintainability
content:
# Code Architecture

Define responsibilities per module; minimize cyclic dependencies. Prefer explicit interfaces over shared globals. Isolate IO and side effects behind adapters. Evolve in small steps with migration paths. Validate designs against change scenarios (new feature, new client, scaling). Keep diagrams lightweight and current.

name: Unit Testing
description: Fast, isolated tests for pure logic and small components
content:
# Unit Testing

Test behavior via public APIs; mock only slow or non-deterministic edges. One logical assertion theme per test name. Use factories/fixtures to reduce duplication without hiding intent. Keep tests deterministic—control time, randomness, and filesystem. Failures should pinpoint the broken rule in one glance.

name: Integration Testing
description: Verify components together with realistic boundaries
content:
# Integration Testing

Choose real dependencies that matter (DB, queue) and fake the rest. Seed minimal data per test; clean up reliably. Assert on externally visible outcomes, not internal calls. Run in CI with containers where possible. Keep suites parallel-friendly and time-bounded to avoid flakes.

name: End-to-End Testing
description: User journeys across the full stack in realistic environments
content:
# E2E Testing

Pick critical user paths and stable selectors. Stabilize with waits on state, not arbitrary sleeps. Isolate test accounts and data to prevent collisions. Capture artifacts on failure (video, trace, logs). Keep the suite lean—push fast checks downward to unit/integration layers.

name: Web Testing
description: Cross-browser checks for functional and visual quality
content:
# Web Testing

Define environment (base URL, auth) up front. Cover navigation, forms, and critical API-backed views. Validate accessibility basics alongside function. Use network mocking sparingly—prefer contract tests for APIs. Parallelize safely with isolated sessions. Investigate flakes with traces, not retries alone.

name: Mobile Testing
description: Simulator and device validation for native mobile apps
content:
# Mobile Testing

Test on representative OS versions and screen sizes. Exercise permissions, backgrounding, and offline modes where relevant. Validate deep links, push handling, and upgrades. Capture screenshots/logs for failures. Automate happy paths; keep exploratory passes for UX edge cases.

name: API Testing
description: Contract, negative, and performance checks for HTTP APIs
content:
# API Testing

Cover authn/authz matrix: valid, missing, and wrong tokens. Assert status, headers, and schema shape. Include pagination, filtering, and idempotency behaviors. Test rate limits and error models. Version-sensitive clients deserve compatibility suites. Store fixtures as data, not copy-paste.

name: Load Testing
description: Stress and capacity validation under realistic traffic
content:
# Load Testing

Model user workflows, think time, and payload distributions. Ramp gradually; watch p95/p99 and error rates. Identify bottlenecks (DB, CPU, locks) with profiling. Run from an environment that mirrors prod topology enough to matter. Document SLOs tested and breaking points observed.

name: Visual Regression
description: Pixel and perceptual diffs to catch unintended UI drift
content:
# Visual Regression

Stabilize fonts, animations, and timestamps in snapshots. Use consistent viewport and theme states. Review intentional changes by updating baselines with scrutiny. Scope snapshots to components when possible to reduce noise. Pair with accessibility checks for text contrast regressions.

name: Accessibility Audit
description: WCAG-oriented checks for inclusive interfaces
content:
# Accessibility Audit

Keyboard-navigate every flow; focus order must make sense. Verify labels, roles, and live regions for dynamic content. Check color contrast and text scaling. Test with a screen reader on primary tasks. Prefer semantic HTML/native controls over custom widgets. Log issues with severity and repro steps.

name: Test Planning
description: Risk-based coverage planning before implementation spikes
content:
# Test Planning

Identify risks by impact and likelihood. Map requirements to test types (unit/integration/e2e). Define entry/exit criteria and environments. Estimate data, tools, and automation feasibility. Align with release cadence and hotfix paths. Revisit plan when scope shifts materially.

name: Exploratory Testing
description: Chartered sessions to find unknown-unknowns quickly
content:
# Exploratory Testing

Time-box missions with clear charters and notes. Vary inputs, orderings, and state combinations. Watch for error handling, recovery, and data integrity. Capture minimal repros with evidence. Debrief findings into tickets with severity and suspected component. Complement—don’t replace—automation.

name: Regression Suite
description: Curate automated checks that protect core user value
content:
# Regression Suite

Prioritize tests that catch frequent breakages and costly failures. Remove redundant cases that don’t increase signal. Keep runtime within CI budgets via sharding and tagging. Fail fast on main branch protections. Review flaky tests weekly; fix or quarantine with owners.

name: Docker Containerization
description: Reproducible images, layers, and local dev parity
content:
# Docker Containerization

Multi-stage builds to keep images small; pin base digests for supply-chain stability. Run as non-root; avoid baking secrets into layers. Use healthchecks and explicit exposed ports. Document build args and runtime env vars. Validate locally and in CI with the same compose file when possible.

name: Kubernetes Deployment
description: Rollouts, resources, and cluster-safe configuration
content:
# Kubernetes Deployment

Declare desired state; avoid imperative drift. Set requests/limits from measured usage. Use readiness/liveness probes thoughtfully. Manage secrets via external stores, not plain manifests in git. Plan rollbacks and progressive delivery. Test changes in a staging cluster matching prod networking policies.

name: CI Pipeline
description: Fast feedback loops for build, test, and artifact promotion
content:
# CI Pipeline

Split fast checks from slow suites; fail early on lint/type/test. Cache dependencies deterministically. Sign artifacts and store provenance. Gate merges on required checks; keep flakes at zero tolerance. Document how to reproduce CI locally. Monitor queue times and optimize bottlenecks.

name: CD Deployment
description: Safe promotion from staging to production with observability
content:
# CD Deployment

Automate promotion with human gates only where policy demands. Use feature flags for risky changes. Verify migrations backward-compatible or paired with code. Watch error budgets and canary metrics post-deploy. Keep rollback one command away. Document customer-facing changes in release notes.

name: Infrastructure as Code
description: Declarative environments with reviewable changes
content:
# Infrastructure as Code

Treat infra changes like application PRs: plan, review, apply. Modularize to reduce blast radius. Pin providers/modules and document assumptions. Separate environments with clear variable layers. Run drift detection periodically. Backup state carefully and restrict access.

name: Monitoring Setup
description: Dashboards, alerts, and SLOs that match on-call reality
content:
# Monitoring Setup

Instrument golden signals: latency, traffic, errors, saturation. Alert on user-impacting symptoms, not every threshold twitch. Use SLOs and burn rates for paging policy. Dashboards should answer: what broke, for whom, since when. Tag consistently across services for correlation.

name: Log Analysis
description: Search, correlate, and explain incidents from log evidence
content:
# Log Analysis

Start from a time window and correlation id. Filter progressively; avoid overly broad queries in prod. Compare canary vs baseline when applicable. Extract patterns: new error fingerprints, dependency failures, auth anomalies. Summarize timeline, blast radius, and likely root cause with citations.

name: Incident Response
description: Coordinate mitigation, communication, and postmortems
content:
# Incident Response

Declare severity and assign roles (incident lead, comms). Stabilize first—rollback, scale, or feature-disable. Communicate status on a predictable cadence. Capture timestamps, actions, and decisions. After resolution, run a blameless postmortem with action items and owners.

name: Environment Management
description: Dev/stage/prod parity and configuration discipline
content:
# Environment Management

Minimize special-case behavior between envs; prefer feature flags over forks. Document required secrets and their rotation. Keep data policies explicit (PII in prod only). Validate migrations across env order. Automate provisioning to reduce snowflake machines. Test disaster recovery periodically.

name: Secrets Management
description: Secure storage, rotation, and least-privilege access
content:
# Secrets Management

Never commit secrets; scan repos and pre-commit hooks help. Store in a vault/KMS with audit logs. Rotate on compromise and on schedule for long-lived creds. Scope tokens narrowly (read-only where possible). Inject at runtime; avoid printing in logs. Document break-glass procedures.

name: React Patterns
description: Hooks, composition, and performance-minded component design
content:
# React Patterns

Prefer composition over inheritance; keep components small and focused. Memoize only when profiling shows benefit—avoid premature useMemo/useCallback. Lift state minimally; colocate state that changes together. Handle effects with correct dependencies and cleanup. Suspense/error boundaries for resilience.

name: Vue Patterns
description: Reactivity, composition API, and scalable SFC structure
content:
# Vue Patterns

Model state close to usage; derive computed values instead of duplicating. Extract composables for cross-cutting concerns. Keep components dumb where possible via props/events. Type your props and emits in TS. Watch for unnecessary deep reactivity on large objects. Test logic in composables independently.

name: CSS Architecture
description: Scalable styling with predictable specificity and tokens
content:
# CSS Architecture

Prefer design tokens for color/type/spacing. Avoid deep selector chains; use class conventions (BEM/CSS modules). Co-locate styles with components when it aids locality. Document theming and dark mode rules. Keep global CSS minimal and intentional. Audit unused rules periodically.

name: Responsive Design
description: Layouts that adapt across breakpoints and input modes
content:
# Responsive Design

Design mobile-first; add complexity as width grows. Test touch targets and hover-less interactions. Prefer fluid type/spacing with sensible clamps. Handle safe areas and keyboard overlap on mobile web. Validate reading order independent of visual layout. Check performance on low-end devices.

name: Component Design
description: Reusable UI primitives with clear APIs and states
content:
# Component Design

Define props for variants, size, and disabled/loading/error states. Document examples and a11y expectations. Avoid leaking internal DOM unless necessary. Keep side effects explicit and testable. Version breaking API changes carefully. Prefer tokens over magic numbers for spacing and color.

name: State Management
description: Predictable data flow for complex client applications
content:
# State Management

Choose local state by default; lift only when multiple consumers need it. Normalize remote data to prevent duplication. Model async with explicit status fields, not implicit coupling. Avoid storing derived values unless caching is measured. Debuggability beats cleverness—keep actions named and traceable.

name: Web Performance
description: Core Web Vitals and bundle discipline for fast sites
content:
# Web Performance

Measure real user metrics, not just lab scores once. Optimize critical path: fonts, images, JS budget. Code-split routes and heavy widgets. Preload only what’s high-confidence. Fix layout shifts by reserving space. Cache aggressively but validate invalidation on deploy.

name: PWA Development
description: Installable web apps with offline and update strategies
content:
# PWA Development

Service workers should be versioned and safe to update; avoid stale caches. Define offline fallbacks for key routes. Request permissions contextually. Test install prompts and deep links across browsers. Keep security headers strict—HTTPS only. Audit storage quotas and cleanup policies.

name: Design System
description: Tokens, documentation, and governance for UI consistency
content:
# Design System

Codify tokens and components with clear contribution rules. Provide usage guidance and do/don’t examples. Deprecate with migration paths, not silent breaks. Measure adoption and drift in product repos. Pair design and engineering reviews for new primitives. Test a11y and themes systematically.

name: UI Animation
description: Motion that aids comprehension without harming performance
content:
# UI Animation

Animate purpose: orientation, feedback, or delight—not decoration alone. Respect reduced-motion preferences. Prefer transform/opacity; avoid layout-thrashing properties. Keep durations and easing consistent with system patterns. Profile on low-end hardware. Provide skip or instant modes where appropriate.

name: REST API Implementation
description: Resource-oriented HTTP APIs with consistent semantics
content:
# REST API Implementation

Use nouns for resources; verbs live in HTTP methods. Return correct status codes and problem details for errors. Support pagination, filtering, and stable sorting contracts. Validate input at the boundary. Version when compatibility promises require it. Document examples and auth scopes.

name: GraphQL API
description: Schema design, resolvers, and operational safeguards
content:
# GraphQL API

Design schema for evolution: nullable fields and additive changes. Prevent N+1 with batching/dataloaders. Enforce depth/complexity limits and timeouts. Authz must be field-aware, not only route-level. Instrument slow resolvers. Provide persisted queries or codegen for clients when scale demands.

name: Authentication
description: Identity flows, sessions, tokens, and threat modeling
content:
# Authentication

Prefer standard protocols (OAuth2/OIDC) over bespoke crypto. Hash passwords with modern algorithms and unique salts. Rotate signing keys and handle clock skew. Bind tokens to audience and issuer. Add MFA where risk warrants. Log auth failures without storing secrets. Test revocation paths.

name: Caching Strategy
description: Correctness-first caching across CDN, app, and data layers
content:
# Caching Strategy

Name keys and TTL policies explicitly; avoid implicit defaults. Invalidate on writes or use versioned keys. Protect against stampedes with locking or probabilistic early expiry. Measure hit rates and p95 improvements. Never cache user-specific data without strict key scoping and auth checks.

name: Database Migration
description: Safe, reversible schema and data changes at scale
content:
# Database Migration

Expand-contract for zero-downtime: add new columns/tables, dual-write/read, then remove old. Backfill in batches with throttling. Test rollback on copies of prod-sized data. Lock risks: prefer online schema tools. Document operational runbook and expected duration. Verify indexes concurrently where supported.

name: Queue Processing
description: Reliable async work with retries, idempotency, and DLQs
content:
# Queue Processing

Make consumers idempotent; dedupe with keys where needed. Define retry policy with jitter and max attempts. Dead-letter queues require triage playbooks. Monitor age-of-oldest message and processing rates. Keep payloads small; store blobs externally. Test failure injection regularly.

name: Microservices
description: Service boundaries, contracts, and operational tradeoffs
content:
# Microservices

Split only when independent scaling/teams justify overhead. Define APIs and failure modes explicitly. Avoid synchronous chains; prefer async where possible. Standardize observability: tracing across services. Data ownership per service—no shared DB as integration point. Practice graceful degradation.

name: Serverless
description: Functions and managed platforms with cold starts in mind
content:
# Serverless

Keep handlers small; externalize config via env. Mind cold starts—trim dependencies and initialization. Set timeouts and concurrency limits deliberately. Use idempotency for retried invocations. Centralize logging/metrics with correlation ids. Test IAM least privilege and secret injection paths.

name: WebSocket Services
description: Real-time channels, backpressure, and auth at connection time
content:
# WebSocket Services

Authenticate during handshake; re-auth on token refresh if long-lived. Handle ping/pong and idle disconnects. Apply backpressure; drop or sample under load. Namespace channels carefully; authorize per subscription. Test reconnect storms and ordering guarantees. Log connection lifecycle metrics.

name: Rate Limiting
description: Fair usage, abuse protection, and client-visible errors
content:
# Rate Limiting

Choose algorithm to match fairness (token bucket vs fixed window). Key by user, tenant, and IP as appropriate. Return 429 with Retry-After and stable error codes. Protect shared dependencies (DB, partners) first. Monitor false positives and adjust. Document limits in API docs.

name: iOS (Swift)
description: SwiftUI/UIKit patterns, concurrency, and App Store readiness
content:
# iOS (Swift)

Prefer Swift 6 concurrency rules; isolate UI on MainActor. Model navigation state clearly; avoid massive view bodies—extract subviews. Handle permissions and background modes explicitly. Test on multiple OS versions and Dynamic Type. Profile memory and launch time early. Follow HIG for platform expectations.

name: Android (Kotlin)
description: Compose/material guidance, lifecycle, and device fragmentation
content:
# Android (Kotlin)

Use lifecycle-aware collectors; avoid leaking Composition. Model UI state in a single source of truth. Handle configuration changes and process death. Test across OEM quirks and API levels. Minimize main-thread IO; prefer structured concurrency. Secure storage for tokens; follow backup rules.

name: React Native
description: Bridge boundaries, native modules, and release hardening
content:
# React Native

Profile JS thread vs native; watch for unnecessary re-renders. Prefer platform files when UX diverges. Version native dependencies carefully; rebuild pods/gradle lockfiles. Test deep links, push, and OTA update policies if used. Add error reporting with symbolicated stacks. Validate performance on low-end Android.

name: Flutter
description: Widgets, platform channels, and build/release pipelines
content:
# Flutter

Keep widgets pure; lift ephemeral state appropriately. Use keys when lists reorder. Isolate platform channels behind small APIs. Test on iOS and Android typography/semantics differences. Watch build size and shader warmup issues. Integrate crash reporting with symbolicated symbols for both stores.

name: Mobile CI
description: Build, sign, and test mobile apps in automated pipelines
content:
# Mobile CI

Cache dependencies deterministically; pin Xcode/NDK images. Secure signing material via secret stores and ephemeral keychains. Run unit/UI tests on simulators with flake controls. Produce artifacts with metadata: commit, version, mapping files. Gate releases on smoke tests and lint rules.

name: App Store Submission
description: Compliance, metadata, and release checklist for stores
content:
# App Store Submission

Verify privacy nutrition labels and permission strings match behavior. Prepare screenshots and review notes for complex flows. Test IAP/receipt validation paths. Monitor rejection patterns; fix policy issues at root. Coordinate phased rollout and crash dashboards post-launch.

name: OWASP Top 10
description: Practical mitigation patterns for common web vulnerabilities
content:
# OWASP Top 10

Validate and encode data at trust boundaries. Use parameterized queries; never concatenate SQL. Enforce authz on every endpoint, not just UI hiding. Protect against SSRF with egress controls. Secure headers, cookies (HttpOnly/SameSite), and CSRF where cookies authenticate. Threat-test file uploads and deserialization.

name: Threat Modeling
description: STRIDE-style analysis to prioritize security work
content:
# Threat Modeling

Diagram trust boundaries and data flows. Identify assets and adversaries realistic to your product. Score risks by exploitability and impact; track mitigations with owners. Revisit on major architecture changes. Prefer eliminating attack surface over adding controls. Document assumptions explicitly.

name: Penetration Testing
description: Goal-oriented offensive testing with safe scope
content:
# Penetration Testing

Define scope, rules of engagement, and emergency contacts. Focus on high-impact chains: cred theft, privilege escalation, data exfil. Record repro steps and evidence clearly. Differentiate findings by severity with remediation guidance. Retest fixes; watch for variant bypasses.

name: Security Code Audit
description: Line-by-line review for dangerous patterns and misuse
content:
# Security Code Audit

Hunt for injection, unsafe deserialization, weak crypto, and authz gaps. Check secret handling, logging redaction, and error verbosity. Review dependency usage of risky APIs. Validate file/path operations and SSRF-prone HTTP clients. Pair findings with tests that prevent regressions.

name: API Security
description: Hardening HTTP/GraphQL endpoints against abuse and data leaks
content:
# API Security

Authenticate consistently; authorize per resource instance. Rate limit and detect credential stuffing patterns. Validate content types and payload sizes. Prevent IDOR by scoping queries to tenant/user. Log security events with correlation, not sensitive payloads. Fuzz inputs for parser edge cases.

name: Dependency Scanning
description: Automated SBOM and vulnerability management in CI
content:
# Dependency Scanning

Fail builds on criticals with known fixes; triage others on SLA. Review transitive risks, not only direct deps. Combine version upgrades with test coverage. Maintain an SBOM export for compliance asks. Track license obligations. Escalate repeated suppressions for architectural fixes.

name: Secrets Detection
description: Prevent credential leaks in repos, logs, and artifacts
content:
# Secrets Detection

Run pre-commit and CI scanners; block merges on high confidence. Rotate any leaked credential immediately—assume compromise. Reduce false positives with allowlists tied to owners. Audit build logs and crash reports for accidental dumps. Educate on .env hygiene and local tooling defaults.

name: Compliance Review
description: Map controls to frameworks and evidence for audits
content:
# Compliance Review

Translate legal/policy requirements into concrete controls. Collect evidence: policies, tickets, access reviews, logs. Identify gaps with owners and timelines. Avoid checkbox theater—tie controls to real risks. Prepare auditor-friendly narratives and diagrams. Reassess after major product changes.

name: SQL Optimization
description: Query plans, indexes, and workload-aware tuning
content:
# SQL Optimization

Measure with EXPLAIN (ANALYZE) on production-like data sizes. Add indexes for selective predicates; avoid over-indexing writes. Rewrite N+1 patterns into joins or batching. Update statistics and validate planner assumptions. Cache only when correctness is preserved. Document hot queries and SLO targets.

name: Data Modeling
description: Entities, relationships, and integrity constraints that age well
content:
# Data Modeling

Start from access patterns, not diagrams alone. Normalize to reduce anomalies; denormalize deliberately with invariants. Choose keys that stabilize across systems. Document cardinality and lifecycle rules. Plan migrations for evolving requirements. Validate with realistic fixtures and constraint tests.

name: ETL Pipeline
description: Extract, transform, load with idempotency and data quality gates
content:
# ETL Pipeline

Define SLAs and freshness; track lag metrics. Make stages idempotent to survive retries. Validate schemas and row counts between steps. Handle late-arriving data explicitly. Partition for scale; avoid full-table rescans. Document lineage from source to consumption for debugging.

name: Analytics Dashboard
description: Metrics that drive decisions without misleading aggregates
content:
# Analytics Dashboard

Clarify the question each chart answers. Prefer cohorts and denominators to vanity totals. Document definitions and data freshness. Guard against survivorship and Simpson’s paradox. Add drill-downs for investigations. Test filters and permissions for multi-tenant data.

name: Data Validation
description: Constraints, checks, and monitoring for trustworthy datasets
content:
# Data Validation

Validate at ingestion boundaries with explicit schemas. Monitor distributions for drift and anomalies. Quarantine bad batches instead of silent drops. Tie validations to ownership and alerting. Sample-audit periodically with human review for edge cases. Document expected ranges and null semantics.

name: Migration Planning
description: Cross-system moves with cutover, rollback, and parity checks
content:
# Migration Planning

Inventory sources, mappings, and transformation rules. Run parallel writes/reads with reconciliation reports. Practice cutover in staging with timers. Define rollback triggers and data repair steps. Communicate freeze windows and customer impact. Verify counts, sums, and spot-check samples post-migration.

name: Sprint Planning
description: Capacity-based commitments with clear definitions of done
content:
# Sprint Planning

Groom backlog to ready stories with acceptance criteria. Size using team history, not optimism. Protect capacity for tech debt and incidents. Align on sprint goal and explicit non-goals. Identify dependencies and risks early. Close with a testable plan and owner per item.

name: Requirements Writing
description: Unambiguous specs that separate problem, constraints, and solution
content:
# Requirements Writing

State user outcomes and measurable acceptance tests. Separate must/should/could; note assumptions explicitly. Capture edge cases, permissions, and failure behavior. Link to designs and analytics events when relevant. Iterate with engineering early to surface unknowns. Avoid solution dictation unless constrained.

name: User Stories
description: Small vertical slices with personas and testable value
content:
# User Stories

Write as: role, need, benefit. Keep stories independently valuable where possible. Add acceptance criteria as checklists, not essays. Split spikes when uncertainty is high. Map non-functional needs (perf, security) explicitly. Review in backlog refinement before sprint start.

name: Roadmap Creation
description: Now/next/later planning aligned to strategy and dependencies
content:
# Roadmap Creation

Anchor on outcomes and metrics, not feature lists alone. Sequence by dependencies, risk reduction, and learning milestones. Communicate uncertainty with time horizons, not false precision. Revisit quarterly; cut mercilessly when priorities shift. Connect roadmap items to customer evidence and support load.

name: Retrospective
description: Facilitated reflection that produces owned improvements
content:
# Retrospective

Create safety: focus on systems, not individuals. Gather data, then patterns—avoid solutioning too early. Pick 1–3 experiments with owners and dates. Track previous action items honestly. Rotate facilitation styles to reduce staleness. Close with appreciation and clarity on next steps.

name: Stakeholder Communication
description: Clear updates, tradeoffs, and decision records
content:
# Stakeholder Communication

Lead with decisions needed or outcomes achieved. Summarize risks with mitigations and dates. Use consistent formats: goal, progress, blockers, asks. Avoid jargon; define acronyms once. Document agreements after meetings. Follow up when timelines slip—early surprises beat late ones.

name: Technical RFC
description: Design proposals with alternatives and rollout plans
content:
# Technical RFC

State problem, constraints, and non-goals. Present options with tradeoffs; recommend one with rationale. Include migration, testing, and observability plans. Identify reviewers and security/privacy impacts. Set decision deadline and status (proposed/accepted/deprecated). Update doc as implementation learns.

name: ADR Writing
description: Lightweight architecture decision records for future context
content:
# ADR Writing

Record context, decision, and consequences—keep it short. Number ADRs sequentially; supersede rather than edit history. Link to code, tickets, and metrics that motivated change. Note rejected alternatives briefly. Revisit when assumptions change. Store close to the codebase for discoverability.

name: README Writing
description: Onboarding docs that get newcomers productive fast
content:
# README Writing

Start with what it is, who it’s for, and how to run locally in minutes. List prerequisites, env vars, and common commands. Link to deeper docs rather than duplicating policies. Include troubleshooting for known footguns. Keep badges accurate; update when scripts change.

name: API Documentation
description: Accurate examples, errors, and versioning guidance
content:
# API Documentation

Document auth, rate limits, and pagination patterns up front. Provide request/response examples for happy and common error paths. Keep schemas generated from source when possible. Version docs alongside API versions. Add changelog entries for breaking changes with migration notes.

name: Changelog Maintenance
description: User-facing release notes with clear upgrade guidance
content:
# Changelog Maintenance

Follow Keep a Changelog style: Added/Changed/Fixed/Removed/Security. Link PRs/issues when helpful. Call out migrations and config changes loudly. Align version bumps with semver rules. Automate generation where possible but curate for humans. Proofread for customer impact language.

name: Runbook Authoring
description: Operational procedures for incidents and routine tasks
content:
# Runbook Authoring

Write steps a tired on-call engineer can follow. Include verification commands and expected outputs. Link dashboards, logs queries, and escalation paths. Document rollback for risky changes. Test runbooks during game days. Keep ownership and last-reviewed date visible.

name: Onboarding Guide
description: Role-based first-week plans for engineers and contributors
content:
# Onboarding Guide

Split by role: app, infra, data. Day-one: access, laptop setup, repo clone, hello-world PR. Week-one: architecture tour, coding standards, on-call shadowing if applicable. List mentors and where to ask questions. Update quarterly; remove stale tooling steps.

name: Peer Collaboration
description: Patterns for multi-agent coordination and shared context
content:
# Peer Collaboration

Establish shared goals, boundaries, and handoff formats between agents. Prefer explicit summaries over implicit state. Avoid duplicate work with clear task ownership. Use human-visible checkpoints for risky decisions. When parallelizing, define merge order and conflict resolution. Close loops with outcomes and open questions.

name: Blackboard Patterns
description: Shared knowledge store usage for facts, tasks, and decisions
content:
# Blackboard Patterns

Write atomic entries with keys that age well; include provenance and timestamps. Prefer structured JSON over prose blobs when machines consume it. Define TTL or archival rules for stale facts. Avoid overwriting without noting supersession. Link entries to conversations and owners for traceability.

name: Delegation Patterns
description: Split work across specialists with crisp inputs and outputs
content:
# Delegation Patterns

Package context: goal, constraints, artifacts, and definition of done. Keep delegations small enough to verify. Pass structured artifacts (diffs, test output) back upstream. Escalate ambiguities early rather than guessing. Record decisions so the parent agent can resume safely.

name: Workspace Collaboration
description: Shared directories, permissions, and safe parallel edits
content:
# Workspace Collaboration

Agree on directory ownership and naming conventions. Use branches or subfolders to reduce merge conflicts. Document required tools and env files without committing secrets. Validate file locks or coordination when automation collides. Snapshot critical state before destructive operations.

name: Agent Identity
description: Roles, prompts, and permissions that stay consistent over time
content:
# Agent Identity

Define the agent’s mission, tools, and escalation rules up front. Keep prompts stable; version changes intentionally. Align permissions with least privilege for the task. Disclose limitations and confidence to users. Avoid persona drift by re-grounding on the system template periodically.

name: Web Scraping
description: Respectful extraction with parsing, rate limits, and legality
content:
# Web Scraping

Check robots/terms; prefer official APIs when available. Throttle requests; backoff on errors; identify your client ethically. Parse defensively—sites change. Store minimal data and redact PII. Handle encodings and pagination robustly. Monitor for breakage with structured alerts.

name: Browser Automation
description: Reliable UI automation with stable selectors and debugging
content:
# Browser Automation

Prefer resilient selectors; avoid brittle XPath tied to layout. Stabilize with auto-waits; never hard-sleep unless unavoidable. Capture traces on failure. Parameterize environments and credentials safely. Keep sessions isolated for parallel runs. Document flaky tests with repro recipes.

name: Image Processing
description: Resize, compress, and transform images without quality surprises
content:
# Image Processing

Choose formats intentionally: lossless vs lossy tradeoffs. Preserve metadata policy explicitly (strip EXIF when needed). Generate responsive variants with consistent naming. Validate color profiles for web. For ML/vision tasks, document prompts and safety constraints. Benchmark CPU/memory for batch jobs.

name: PDF Generation
description: Layout-faithful documents with fonts, pagination, and accessibility
content:
# PDF Generation

Embed fonts or subset carefully; test missing glyph paths. Handle pagination, headers/footers, and tables that split across pages. Add tags for accessibility when required. Secure outputs: redact sensitive fields; avoid executable content surprises. Verify file size and generation time at scale.

name: ClaudPeer Accessibility
description: Accessibility identifier conventions for ClaudPeer UI — enables AppXray and UI automation
content:
# ClaudPeer Accessibility Identifiers

Every interactive or semantically meaningful SwiftUI element must have an `.accessibilityIdentifier()` so AppXray (`@testId()`) and UI automation can target it.

## Naming Convention

Use dot-separated `viewName.elementName` in camelCase:
- Static: `"chat.sendButton"`, `"sidebar.conversationList"`, `"settings.general.appearancePicker"`
- Dynamic rows: append UUID — `"sidebar.conversationRow.\(id.uuidString)"`
- Nested contexts: `"agentEditor.skills.addButton.\(skill.id.uuidString)"`

## What Gets What

| Element Type | Modifiers |
|---|---|
| Button with text label | `.accessibilityIdentifier()` |
| Icon-only Button | `.accessibilityIdentifier()` + `.accessibilityLabel()` |
| TextField / TextEditor | `.accessibilityIdentifier()` |
| Picker / Toggle / Stepper | `.accessibilityIdentifier()` |
| List / ScrollView | `.accessibilityIdentifier()` on the container |
| Dynamic rows | `.accessibilityIdentifier()` with UUID suffix |
| Status indicators | `.accessibilityIdentifier()` + `.accessibilityLabel()` |
| Decorative-only | `.accessibilityElement(children: .ignore)` |

## Existing Prefix Map

| View | Prefix |
|---|---|
| MainWindowView | `mainWindow.*` |
| SidebarView | `sidebar.*` |
| ChatView | `chat.*` |
| InspectorView | `inspector.*` |
| NewSessionSheet | `newSession.*` |
| AgentLibraryView | `agentLibrary.*` |
| AgentEditorView | `agentEditor.*` |
| SettingsView | `settings.general.*`, `settings.connection.*`, `settings.advanced.*` |
| AgentCardView | `agentCard.*` |
| MessageBubble | `messageBubble.*` |
| ToolCallView | `toolCall.*` |
| CodeBlockView | `codeBlock.*` |
| StatusBadge | `statusBadge.*` |
| StreamingIndicator | `streamingIndicator` |
| InfoRow | `infoRow.*` |

## When Adding New Views

1. Pick a unique camelCase prefix matching the view name.
2. Add `.accessibilityIdentifier("prefix.elementName")` to every button, field, picker, toggle, list, and meaningful text.
3. For icon-only buttons, also add `.accessibilityLabel("Human-readable action")`.
4. For dynamic `ForEach` rows, suffix with `.\(item.id.uuidString)`.
5. Never reuse an identifier across different views.

name: Email Templates
description: Cross-client HTML/text with deliverability and compliance in mind
content:
# Email Templates

Provide both HTML and text parts; keep HTML table-layout simple for clients. Inline critical CSS where needed; test dark mode. Include unsubscribe/manage prefs for marketing contexts. Validate links and tracking parameters. Warm up sending domains; monitor bounces and spam complaints.
